documents.topic: Empirical survey of five candidate naming patterns for the Windows-side L2/L3 platform stack, triggered by Path X close (2026-04-30) and the user direction that future strict-platform compliance phases raw forms out at L2 entirely (typed-only L2 surface). Five variants tested as 3-target SPM shapes: V1 status-quo (Windows.Kernel.X at both L2 and L3), V2 sub-namespace (Windows.ABI.Kernel.X at L2), V3 twin roots (Win32.Kernel.X at L2, mirrors POSIX ISO_9945/POSIX shape), V4 org-prefix (Microsoft.Kernel.X at L2), V5 literal-spec (WinSDK.Kernel.X at L2). All 16 targets build green; differential signal is architectural. Recommends V3 (twin roots) on spec/policy-separation, [API-NAME-003] specification-mirroring, and POSIX-shape-symmetry grounds. Surfaces the open question of whether the existing POSIX = ISO_9945 typealias should break for cross-platform symmetry (principal decision; not autonomously decided).
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: V3 twin roots (Win32.Kernel.X at L2 + Windows.Kernel.X at L3). V1 REFUTED architecturally (no spec/policy boundary; future typed-only L2 collision). V2 sub-optimal (ABI sub-tag is not a Microsoft-published spec authority). V4 REFUTED (org-prefix root contradicts [API-NAME-003] spec-mirroring; ecosystem already roots specs at the spec name, not the publisher). V5 REFUTED (Windows-host-only module-vs-type collision invisible to macOS/Linux CI). Open question on POSIX-side typealias-break surfaced for principal decision.

Pre-flight escalation: 9 additional engagement+ingest skills (`swift-institute/Engagement/Skills/`) accessible via .claude/skills symlinks but explicitly out of brief scope (private sibling repo per `feedback_skills_follow_institute_convention.md`); flagged in §28.3 as scope-expansion candidate, NOT classified per supervisor MUST clause. Walks 544 requirement IDs across 24 skills (mean 22.7/skill); aggregate 263 mechanical / 146 hybrid / 135 semantic = 48.3/26.8/24.8%. **Tier-3 mechanical density (48%) is comparable to tier-2 (51%), NOT "dominantly semantic" as the brief expected** — operational sub-clusters (testing-* 80–100%, dutch-law/legal-encoding 55–86%, document-markup 76%, swift-pull-request 82%) maintain a robust mechanical floor offsetting the semantic-heavy supervise (26%) and research-process (16%) clusters. Semantic share rebounds modestly (+4.1 pp vs tier-2). γ-roadmap saturation: 28/263 mechanical rules (10.6%) carry γ-marks (26 γ-2 in readme/ci-automation cluster + 1 γ-1b + 1 γ-1c in placeholder + 1 γ-2 in corpus-meta-analysis META-021); concentrated almost entirely in readme. Most are aspirational (carry **API-Gap** annotation — workflows specified-pending). Resistant set: ~190/544 (35%) raw — highest of any tier — driven by Routing (~50 cases) and Reference (anchor) (~50 cases) cluster expansion at scale. After deducting Reference (anchor) cases that classify cleanly, genuinely-resistant fraction drops to ~25%. **No seventh resistant pattern surfaces** — six-pattern soft ceiling holds across all four dispatches. **Ecosystem-wide closure: 47 skills, 1045 requirements, 531 mechanical / 269 hybrid / 245 semantic = 50.8/25.7/23.4%** — roughly half mechanical, quarter hybrid, quarter semantic. Mechanical-rule construction queue (§28.2) ranks all 47 skills by mechanical count: top 10 carry 220 mechanical rules (41% of total). Active γ cluster (≈80 rules with designed checks) feeds immediate workflow-construction substrate; remaining ≈451 mechanical rules form latent reservoir awaiting γ-class extension across 5 candidate families (skill-shape audit, test-shape audit, legal-source-fetch, process-discipline, markup-API). Density outliers triggered: testing-institute & quick-commit-and-push-all 100% mech; dutch-law universal **External:** annotation (17/17 rules); supervise 26% mech as judgment-bearing skill; implementation 0% (4 hub axioms only, sibling-file corollary rules out of scope per brief); readme 83-ID outlier driven by hub+sibling structure with 11 reserved-ID slots. skill-lifecycle has 2 template-literal placeholder `### [` headings inside SKILL-CREATE-005's example block, not classifiable rules — 30 real rules walked. Recommendation: adopt pilot+tier-1+tier-2's six annotations unchanged; optionally adopt Reference sub-shape disambiguator; no seventh class needed. Workflow construction phase begins (separate dispatch) with active γ cluster as immediate substrate, latent reservoir as second-priority requiring v1.2.0 §3.4 γ-class extension. Tier-3 closes the classification sweep; no further classification dispatches scheduled. The 9 engagement+ingest skills surfaced as scope-expansion candidate for supervisor consideration but NOT required for sweep closure. Honors supervisor block: no SKILL.md edited, no Verification: fields added, no workflow YAML drafted, no Phase-1 triage queue updates applied inline, scope held to 24 tier-3 skills, no remote pushes, cross-session contamination preserved untouched.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0. Tier-3 (FINAL) classifies 544 requirements across 24 voice/process skills at 48.3/26.8/24.8% mechanical/hybrid/semantic — comparable to tier-2's 51% mech, NOT dominantly semantic as brief expected (operational sub-clusters maintain mech floor). Ecosystem-wide closure: **47 skills, 1045 requirements, 531m/269h/245s = 50.8/25.7/23.4% — roughly half mechanical, quarter each of hybrid and semantic.** γ-saturation 10.6% in tier-3 (concentrated in readme/ci-automation aspirational marks); ecosystem-wide 80/531 mechanical rules (15%) carry active γ matches. Mechanical-rule construction queue ranked across all 47 skills; top 10 carry 41% of all mechanical rules. No seventh resistant pattern; six-annotation soft ceiling holds across all 1045 requirements. Pre-flight escalation surfaced 9 additional engagement+ingest skills out of brief scope; not classified per supervisor MUST. Latent γ-class candidates surfaced for future v1.2.0 §3.4 extension: skill-shape audit (~50 rules), test-shape audit (~30), legal-source-fetch (~40), process-discipline (~20), markup-API (~13). Workflow-construction phase begins as separate dispatch.

documents.topic: Snapshot naming patterns: cross-ecosystem analysis — named vs unnamed snapshots, inline vs file-backed storage, API design across 8 frameworks (swift-snapshot-testing, Jest, Vitest, Insta, cargo-insta, Verify, ApprovalTests.NET, snapshot_testing Ruby). Key finding: no framework supporting both inline and file-backed uses auto-numbering for inline snapshots.

Snapshot testing and inline snapshot testing literature study (Tier 3, v1.1) — SLR (45 sources: Barr oracle taxonomy, Rothermel regression theory, Feathers characterization tests, Myers diff). 14 ecosystems surveyed (Rust insta, OCaml ppx_expect, JS Jest/Vitest, Python inline-snapshot, JVM Selfie, Swift PointFree). Formal semantics (oracle, serializer canonicity, source rewrite, typing rules, soundness theorem). Cognitive Dimensions analysis (inline > file-based on 4 dimensions). Swift 6.2+ enablers (pre-built SwiftSyntax, ST-0009 Attachments, SE-0451 raw identifiers). Infrastructure mapping: optics-primitives (redaction composition algebra), tree-primitives (structural diff via Tree.Keyed), json/xml (canonical serialization), witnesses/dependencies (scoped configuration). 5 priority improvements with revised effort estimates.

Source location unification via primitive decomposition (Tier 2, v3.1) — factors `(line, column)` into `Text.Location`, renames `Source.Location` → `Source.Position` (compact) and `Source.Location.Resolved` → `Source.Location` (display). Fully implemented: eliminated `Test.Source.Location`, `Witness.Unimplemented.Location`, `Parser.Diagnostic.Location`, `JSON.LocatedError` — all use canonical primitives types directly. 7 packages committed.

Span view integration strategy (Tier 3) — how Span should integrate with the Swift Institute sequence ecosystem for closure-free consumption. Span-to-String and Span-to-Array conversion paths. SE-0456/SE-0467/SE-0474 prior art.

SPM nested package publication feasibility (Tier 2) — empirical validation (12 test configurations), prior art survey (10 proposals/pitches, 8 major packages), Swift Forums confirmation. Nested Package.swift rejected (hard SPM limitation). Current three-repo layered model confirmed as only viable architecture. Git subtree evaluation deferred.

Architectural role of swift-*-standard packages after spec implementations split into standards-body orgs (Tier 2, v2.0) — domain shape taxonomy (primitive-first vs standard-first, single-spec vs multi-spec), two purposes (convergence + stability), three-layer model (body orgs → swift-standards → swift-foundations), stable-vs-volatile separation. Recommends keeping all 17 packages in swift-standards/. Withdraws v1.0 "eliminate thin re-exports" recommendation.
Storage and buffer variant abstraction analysis (Tier 3) — SLR (40 sources), container theory, substructural typing, comparative analysis of 5 candidate abstractions

Stream isolation-preserving operators via concrete types (Tier 2) — experiment confirms concrete `AsyncSequence` operator types with `NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault` preserve isolation for sync AND async closures. Recommends ~20 concrete linear operators + existing type-erased concurrent operators.

Stream isolation propagation analysis (Tier 2) — every operator breaks isolation (100% breakage). Root causes: `@Sendable` `_next`, sync closures cannot be nonsending, actor state, unstructured Tasks. Option D: accept stream as concurrency boundary.

String and path type unification across ecosystem (Tier 3, v2.0) — SLR (16 sources), formal semantics, prior art (Rust/C++/Go/Zig/Python/Swift). 5 types across 3 layers. Experiment `phantom-tagged-string-unification` (9 variants, ALL CONFIRMED) **refuted** C2 blocker — Option D is feasible today. Two viable paths: Option E (refine, conservative) or Option D (phantom-tag, progressive). Decision deferred to design judgment.

String_Primitives.String shadowing Swift.String (Tier 2) — @_exported chain propagates bare `String` type through Kernel to IO/File/Async, breaking withTaskGroup type inference. Type and name are legitimate (ecosystem pattern: Array, Dictionary, Set also shadow). Problem is structural: Kernel_Primitives_Core @_exports String_Primitives despite not using it. Recommends removing @_exported from Core + demoting Kernel_String_Primitives from umbrella (~6 file changes). Follow-up: audit all @_exported chains for same pattern.

What Tag for String_Primitives.String under D' migration (Tier 2) — consumer inventory across 3 layers. Resolved: Tag is always the consumer's domain (Kernel, Loader), concrete PlatformString is the RawValue

Swift 6.4-dev compatibility catalog (Tier 2) — systematic catalogue of 6.4-dev compatibility issues in swift-primitives: `@_lifetime` on Escapable rejected, static property resolution changes, identity closure IRGen crashes, `@_lifetime` version skew for ~Escapable self methods, DeinitDevirtualizer SIL assertion. Categorize by fix type and dual-compiler requirements.
Migrated to `swift-io/Research/audit.md` per [AUDIT-015]. Deleted 2026-03-24.
Optimal format for exporting Swift packages to ChatGPT
Swift Testing data maximization (Tier 2) — unified schema for test artifacts, structured performance/functional output, LLM-actionable test diagnostics

Performance testing infrastructure gaps and fixes (Tier 2) — 4 issues blocking reliable performance testing: compiler crash in release (P0), unattributable diagnostics (P1), cross-suite concurrency interference (P2), missing tabular comparison (P3). All resolved.

Converged plan for swift-translating migration — replace Language enum with BCP47.LanguageTag typealias, split Foundation-dependent code into Translating Platform module, strict one-way dependency graph

SwiftPM build plugins for `-Xfrontend` flags (Tier 2) — can build plugins inject `-Xfrontend -enable-experimental-feature` flags without `.unsafeFlags()`? Currently `.unsafeFlags()` disables SPM dependency resolution for downstream consumers, blocking adoption of packages using experimental features (Lifetimes, RawLayout).

System vs Kernel.System namespace boundary (Tier 2) — prior art survey (BSD sysctl CTL_HW/CTL_KERN, Linux sysfs /sys/devices/system/ vs /sys/kernel/, Go, Rust, Apple swift-system). Finding: hardware facts (CPU count, RAM, page size) universally separated from kernel operations; no precedent puts them under "kernel". Recommends Option D: System as complete typed hardware namespace (System.Processor.Count, System.Memory.Capacity, System.Page.Size), Kernel.System retains only OS-operational queries (Name, Path.Length, sleep).
Tagged extension duplication analysis

How to resolve Kernel.Path/String identity when both wrap Memory.Contiguous\<Char\> (Tier 2) — 7 options (A–G). Decision: Option G — concrete types as RawValue, domain as Tag. Kind × Domain = two orthogonal axes. Validated by tagged-two-level-lifetime (6/6 CONFIRMED)
Can Tagged<Element, Cardinal> prove structural Sendable when Element: Sendable? (Tier 1) — could remove @unchecked from phantom-typed containers

Task-local stack unification (Tier 2, v3.0) — structural unification of L1 Dependency.Scope and L3 Witness.Context @TaskLocal stacks. Triggered by architectural dissatisfaction with closure-chain bridging mechanism during Phase 3 L1 Key Bridge implementation.

Temporal operator determinism (Tier 2) — NonsendingClock and deterministic temporal testing, triggered by Pointfree Episode #355. ImmediateClock with nonisolated(nonsending) sleep achieves 100% deterministic testing (10,000 runs, 0 failures).

Test console output: from functional to elegant (Tier 2) — comprehensive analysis of console output across unit tests, performance benchmarks, and snapshot tests. Current state: flat streaming with no hierarchy, no suite aggregation, mixed formatting vocabularies. Apple: experimental AdvancedConsoleOutputRecorder with deferred tree rendering. Prior art: Jest, criterion.rs, hyperfine. Theoretical ideal: 6-phase plan — suite-level aggregation + tree summary (Phase 1), right-aligned durations + terminal width (Phase 2), box-drawn performance tables (Phase 3), real-time suite progress (Phase 4), side-by-side benchmark comparison (Phase 5), comparison CLI (Phase 6). Hybrid reporter architecture (Option C) recommended.
documents.statusDetail: Analysis complete; Option C (Hybrid reporter architecture) recommended with 6 implementation phases identified. Phases 1–3 deliver the most value. Awaiting principal authorization to begin implementation.

documents.topic: Test output quality: parity with Apple and beyond (Tier 2) — 7 improvements identified. Root cause: ConsoleSink discards all Test.Text styling, ignores Failure.expected/actual/difference/comment, omits source location. All data already captured in primitives types. Phase 1: ANSI color + source location + structured fields in reporter (~60 lines, 1 file). Phase 2: pass DiffResult as Failure.difference instead of string-embedding. Phase 3: type-aware sourceCode. After Phase 1-3: parity on all Apple features, exceeding on structural diffs, semantic styling, extensibility.
Test support snapshot strategy sharing (Tier 2) — cross-package sharing of swift-testing-dependent Test.Snapshot.Strategy extensions between parent and nested test support layers
Testing conventions for Swift Institute ecosystem (Tier 3)

Testing discovery revalidation (Swift 6.2.4) — backticked names, nested suites, deep nesting all work. Generic type extension nesting still broken (#1508). ~653 compound test suite names across ecosystem need renaming. No skill updates required.

Time API naming literature review (Tier 2) — Bloch, Fowler, Muratori, Schankin et al. (ICPC 2018), SE-0329 review threads. Nested accessor vs compound identifier analysis. Empirical gap: no study compares `x.in.seconds` vs `x.inSeconds`

Cross-language time/duration API survey (Tier 2) — 7 languages (Rust, Java, C++, Haskell, Go, Python, Swift stdlib), exact method signatures, prefix taxonomy (`as_`/`to`/`in`/`total`/bare), total-vs-component ambiguity analysis

Transformation domain architecture (Tier 2, v3.2) — three top-level namespaces: Parser, Serializer, Coder. Principled correctness framing. Three packages: swift-parser-primitives (existing), swift-serializer-primitives (renamed from swift-serialization-primitives, + Serializer.Protocol + Builder), swift-coder-primitives (new). Coder.Protocol independent with separate DecodeFailure/EncodeFailure. `decode`/`encode` naming. Witness.Protocol + Dependency.Key orthogonal. Shadow stdlib Codable. Canonical-witness attachment validated (10/10 CONFIRMED). All questions resolved.

Tree type family organization across 7 ecosystems — tree variants never share common Tree protocol. "Tree" always the adjective (TreeMap = Map). GNU PBDS `basic_branch_tag` is sole partial exception. Structural trees vs map-backing trees are separate concerns.

Trie and keyed-tree SLR (Tier 3) — Kitchenham methodology, 34 sources across 7 languages. RQ1: canonical signature `Trie<K,V>` where key = `[K]`. RQ2: tries classified as maps, never trees. RQ3: Hinze exponent-law derivation. RQ4: keyed trees extremely rare (Boost.PropertyTree only). Child representation taxonomy (7 strategies).
Complete typed infrastructure inventory, tiers 0–15 (Tier 3) — systematic catalog for existing-infrastructure skill rebuild

Typed throws mixed error domains (Tier 1) — 4 options for aggregate functions composing multiple typed-throws sub-operations: unified error enum (A), Either/sum type (B), phase-tagged wrapping (C), foreign re-throw (D). 3 concrete sites: rfc-9112 Request/Response deserializers (6 error types each), iso-8601 DateTime (1 foreign Time.Error). Recommends Option A (unified error enum) for all sites — wrapping sub-phase errors as associated values preserves full type safety, readability, layer isolation.

Typed throws standards inventory — exhaustive grep of all 2316 source files across 94 packages. 124 untyped throws: 122 Codable protocol-mandated (not worth converting), 2 Clock conformances (converted). 70 packages with zero untyped throws.
Unified geometry types — ecosystem-wide design spanning standards, primitives, and foundations
Critique of unified geometry types proposal
Implementation plan for unified geometry types
Research for unified geometry types design

Variant naming audit (Tier 3) — variant naming conventions for data structure types across swift-primitives ecosystem. Academic foundations, variant system definition, layer naming, composition architecture, complete type inventory, copyability/sendability rules, compiler limitations, naming corrections.

Witness enum direction and dual (Tier 1) — should @Witness accept enums now that @Dual generates a strict superset of @Witness enum infrastructure (Scott encoding + prisms + discriminants + extraction)? Macro input domain overlap analysis.

@Witness macro adoption for algebra types (Tier 1) — source-level audit of 14 @frozen witness types across swift-primitives. Evaluates five macro improvements (let closures, _ prefix stripping, firstName labels, skip-init, non-closure properties) against existing manual witness types.

@Witness macro adoption for IO drivers (Tier 1) — investigates whether IO.Event.Driver and IO.Completion.Driver should use @Witness macro. Both already conform to Witness.Protocol and Witness.Key. Assessment: incremental value only.

@Witness macro support for ~Copyable witnesses (Tier 2, v2.0) — 13 experiment variants. Supersedes witness-macro-io-drivers-assessment.md (revises DEFERRED to VIABLE). Three blockers investigated: ~Copyable enum associated values, borrowing/consuming parameter forwarding, feature value proposition.

Should swift-witnesses support ~Copyable/~Escapable witness values? (Tier 3, v2.0) — SLR (31 sources: Rust/Haskell/OCaml DI, substructural type theory, capability-passing). ~Copyable values feasible (Ownership.Shared, SuppressedAssociatedTypes, TaskLocal not a blocker) but not yet justified — no concrete use case for ~Copyable witness values. Additive closure-based API (Option E) ready when needed. Monitor ~Escapable scoped access.

Witness framework: bifurcation theorem, Sendable removal, macro omission pattern. Consolidates witness-noncopyable-nonescapable-support, witness-macro-noncopyable-support-design, witness-protocol-sendable-requirement. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Should Witness.Protocol require Sendable? (Tier 1) — Remove. Sendable is orthogonal to the witness pattern marker. All existing conformances already declare Sendable explicitly. Witness.Key has independent Sendable constraint. Enables Parser.Machine.Compile.Witness conformance.

Witness Result/Outcome unification (Tier 1) — analysis of @Witness-generated Calls, Result (~Copyable), and Outcome types. Sibling vs nested placement, naming collisions with Standard_Library_Extensions.Result, type structure for DI after-observers.

Witnesses ecosystem adoption audit (Tier 1, v3.0) — identifies every opportunity to adopt Witness.Protocol conformance, @Witness macro usage, and Witness.Key registration across the entire ecosystem. Currently limited to algebra packages and Sample.Averaging; IO drivers use pattern but don't conform.

Worklist rendering dispatch for PDF HTML pipeline (Tier 2) — replaces mutual recursion (`renderHTMLView ↔ renderInnerContent`, 18 frames) with single `Stack<Dispatch>` worklist loop (5 frames), composing from `swift-stack-primitives` and `swift-tree-primitives` patterns. Machine primitives evaluated and rejected. 8 ad-hoc functions absorbed into 1 loop. Supersedes `swift-foundations/swift-pdf-html-rendering/Research/iterative-tuple-rendering.md` solution (retains its tuple/cause analysis).

Workspace-wide symbol search for CCLSP (Tier 2) — 6 options evaluated. Key findings: `rootMarkers` in cclsp.json is silently ignored (not in schema), CCLSP sends exactly one workspace folder, `.xcworkspace` not supported by SourceKit-LSP. Recommends Phase 1: set `rootDir` (trivial, enables implicit workspace discovery), Phase 2: contribute `workspaceFolders` support to ktnyt/cclsp, Phase 3: lightweight root Package.swift if progressive discovery insufficient.

Zero-copy event pipeline design using Memory.Pool (Tier 2) — eliminates per-poll Array allocation in IO.Event.Poll.Loop via pooled buffer slots. Phased: Phase 1 (pool + memcpy), Phase 2 (direct-to-pool polling).

Survey of ownership/concurrency primitive combinations that produce SILGen crashes or miscompilation when layered at a single syntactic site. Follow-up from the 2026-04-17 Effect.Continuation.One two-callback workaround; seeks to identify other latent hazards before they fire in consumer code.

Does the L3 platform-policy tier exist for swift-strings, swift-paths, and other L3 unifiers beyond swift-kernel — or is swift-posix structurally unique? Follow-up investigation after [PLAT-ARCH-008e] codification; audits non-kernel L3 unifiers for the same shadow-layering pattern.

Reusable design for multi-repo batch automation handling the full state-axis enumeration — branch state, remote state, working-tree state, gitignore interactions, divergence — without silent failures. Motivated by the 2026-04-18 _index.md → _index.json migration that hit three silent git add failures across 70+ repos.

How swift-institute.org dashboard discovers, aggregates, and filters across ~70+ per-package _index.json corpora with varying schema shapes post-migration. Discovery mechanism, schema harmonization, scope-pill UX, and cold-load performance.

Canonical shape for bridging typed primitives (L1) into specification types (L2) across the ecosystem. Motivating case: Geometry → CSS.Length bridge friction in the landing-page progressive example. Four candidate shapes (conformance, overloads, explicit, bridge package) weighted against layering rules.

Whether RFC_791.IPv4.Address / RFC_4291.IPv6.Address should change internal storage to match POSIX in_addr / in6_addr for zero-copy unification with sockaddr wrappers. Empirical experiment (swift-rfc-4291/Experiments/ipv6-address-alignment) measured current layouts; prior-art survey (Rust, Go, .NET, Python) shows universal rejection of layout compatibility — Rust explicitly abandoned C sockaddr layout in PR #78802. Recommendation: keep host-order storage, fix inaccurate docstrings, compose at API boundary (iso-9945 / windows-standard accept RFC values, marshal into native sockaddr). Motivated by swift-kernel Finding #17 Windows gap.
DocC behavior with @_exported umbrella re-exports and the idiomatic multi-target documentation pattern

Empirical survey (10 packages) of DocC catalog distribution across multi-target Swift packages from swiftlang/apple/pointfreeco. Complements the Tier 2 DocC aggregation research by confirming Option F is novel in the surveyed ecosystem; explains why other packages don't hit the same problem (public import vs @_exported).

Audit of the /readme skill (22 rules [README-001]–[README-022]) against 15 first-class Swift OSS READMEs (Apple + Point-Free + SSWG). Confirms ~70% alignment; prescribes three new meta-rules (Evaluator's Lens, Motivated Examples, Scope Boundary) addressing the maintainer's concrete critiques on swift-property-primitives/README.md, plus five MUST→SHOULD relaxations for over-prescriptive rules (fixed ordering, ASCII diagrams, bold-keyword Key Features, Platform Support table, Error Handling narrowing). Skill v2.0.0 breaking bump recommended. Input for Part B skill update; downstream package-README rewrite is a separate handoff.

documents.topic: Investigate whether a Hasher-pattern witness type for mutation (a Mutable trait protocol exposing a Mutator<Subject> witness) would unlock anything not already covered by swift-optic-primitives + swift-algebra-* + Swift's ~Copyable/~Escapable language features. Six candidate roles enumerated (Transactional, Journaling, Observation, Validation, Functional-update, Structural-edit). Outcome: investigation completed; the two well-shaped gaps (Setter optic, Semilattice algebra) are extensions to existing primitives, not a separate package.
documents.statusDetail: Investigation completed 2026-04-25; recommendation: do NOT ship swift-mutator-primitives. The compositional machinery is already in swift-optic-primitives + swift-algebra-*; the genuine academic gap (handlers for linear/borrowed state with lifetime-bounded witness) is publishable theory but lacks a credible second consumer per [RES-018]. The well-shaped gaps to address: extend swift-optic-primitives with Optic.Setter; consider Algebra.Semilattice for CRDT-merge if/when needed.

documents.topic: Tier-2+ prior-art survey per [RES-021] covering lens/optics (Foster, van Laarhoven, Pickering-Gibbons-Wu, Riley, Linear Haskell), algebraic effects + handlers (Plotkin-Power, Plotkin-Pretnar, Bauer-Pretnar, Koka), linear/affine + capabilities (Wadler 1990, Walker 2005, Mezzo, Cyclone, Tofte-Talpin, BoCa 2025, RustBelt), CRDTs (Shapiro 2011, Burckhardt 2014), FRP (Elliott-Hudak, Yampa, Flapjax), and type-class dictionary witnesses (Wadler-Blott, SE-0185, SE-0206). 31 citations: 26 [Verified: 2026-04-25] against primary sources.
documents.statusDetail: Companion to mutator-type-hasher-pattern-exploration.md. Ecosystem-grounded: each academic anchor mapped against existing swift-optic-primitives, swift-algebra-*, and Swift language-level ~Copyable/~Escapable. Key finding: the academic gap (handlers for linear/borrowed state, lifetime-bounded witness) exactly maps the Mutator design space; existing ecosystem primitives cover the compositional machinery; the missing piece would be a single-shot lifetime-bounded handler witness — but [RES-018] not cleared.

documents.topic: Naming convention investigation: rename strawman Mutatable to Mutable (proper -able adjective of mutate); apply [PKG-NAME-002] Mutator.Protocol pattern; assess no-clash with Ownership.Mutable type. Result preserved as ecosystem reference even though the swift-mutator-primitives package was retired — the naming spine applies to any future ecosystem package occupying mutation-capability territory.

documents.topic: Why a Mutator capability protocol would be a sibling of Carrier rather than a refinement. Phantom-typed Carrier conformers (User.ID, Cardinal, Ordinal) actively reject mutation; Mutable-only types (Ownership.Inout, Atomic) reject Carrier's construction story. Empirical evidence from dual-conformance experiment.
documents.statusDetail: Origin: swift-mutator-primitives/Research/. Relocated to swift-institute/Research/ on 2026-04-25. The orthogonal-vs-refinement reasoning applies to any future Mutator-shape proposal regardless of whether the package emerges. documents.displayName: _modify across the four Copyable × Escapable quadrants (alternative-shape evidence)
documents.topic: Empirical Swift-language findings: (1) `mutating _modify` is NOT a valid protocol property requirement — Swift accepts only get/set; (2) `@_lifetime(&self)` (not `borrow self`) on `_modify` for ~Escapable Self. Findings remain authoritative for any future ecosystem package considering a full-capability mutation protocol.

documents.topic: Two-phase ecosystem inventory + triage for Carrier conformance, Tagged refactor, and `.Protocol` capability-lift adoption across swift-primitives (134) + swift-standards (21) + swift-foundations (137). Phase 1 inventory: 26 hoisted-protocol declarations classified into Carrier-shaped (4: Cardinal, Ordinal, Hash.Value, Affine.Discrete.Vector — last is NEW finding not in capability-lift-pattern.md ecosystem instances), witness-style (13, out per recommendation #6), abstraction-style (7, wrong shape), self-projection-default (1: Ownership.Borrow), pure marker (1: Witness); ~80 Tagged-aliased value carriers; ~7 standalone single-field wrappers; 1 strong ~Copyable Carrier candidate (Property<Tag, Base>). Phase 2 triage: CAN/SHOULD per candidate biased toward apply. documents.topic: Resolves the Phase 2b migration question of whether to phase out per-type capability protocols (Cardinal.`Protocol`, Ordinal.`Protocol`, Affine.Discrete.Vector.`Protocol`) in favor of `Carrier<Underlying>`. Identifies an asymmetry: Cardinal and Affine.Discrete.Vector's operators are Self+Self-shaped (clean Carrier migration); Ordinal.`Protocol`'s typed-advance is Self+Self.Count where Count is per-conformer (Cardinal for bare Ordinal, Tagged<Tag, Cardinal> for Tagged) — Carrier has no Count associatedtype to replicate this. Enumerates seven options (Self.Domain-derived typealias, Carrier refinement, per-type extension, call-site migration, operator overloads, full retention, selective retention). Recommends Option G — Selective Retention: migrate Cardinal and Affine.Discrete.Vector to Carrier extensions (clean), retain Ordinal.`Protocol` as a SIBLING to Carrier (not a refinement, no V2 cost) for typed-advance ergonomics. Cites Recommendation #6 sibling precedent (Hash/Equation/Comparison coexist with Carrier). Articulates a third role beyond capability-lift and witness: `operator-ergonomics protocols` whose role is associatedtype-driven operator dispatch that Carrier's parameterized form cannot replicate. Cross-language prior art: Rust std::ops::Add, Haskell TypeFamilies, Scala dependent types — universal per-impl associated-type pattern.
documents.statusDetail: Implemented 2026-04-26. Cardinal/Affine.Discrete.Vector migrated to Carrier (clean); Ordinal.`Protocol` restored as sibling. Build + 35 ordinal tests + downstream packages green. Recommendation codified as Recommendation #7 in capability-lift-pattern.md v1.2.0.

documents.topic: Investigation of whether Decimal.Exponent / Precision / Payload should integrate with the Carrier ecosystem (Tagged refactor, Carrier conformance, or remain standalone). Empirical baseline: zero `Tagged<_, Decimal.*>` and zero `Carrier<Decimal.*>` usage across the ecosystem [RES-018]. Tagged refactor structurally blocked by: (a) naming convention — no domain-typed phantom Tag (the role-axes within Decimal don't have natural domain names; synthesized `*Tag` suffixes violate API-NAME conventions), (b) literal-conformance gating per tagged-literal-conformances-fresh-perspective.md (Strideable interaction reactivates the cross-domain footgun). Carrier<Int> conformance erases domain identity per [IMPL-001] (treating power-of-10 exponent arithmetic as generic Int erases the domain that motivates the type). Trivial-self-Carrier conformance is premature without second consumer. Recommendation: Decimal types remain standalone struct types — populate a third role-class (domain-specific value types) alongside witness protocols (Rec #6) and operator-ergonomics protocols (Rec #7). Proposes Recommendation #8 for capability-lift-pattern.md codifying this third class. Supersedes inventory v1.0.0's Phase 4 CAN-yes verdict.
documents.statusDetail: Resolves Phase 4 of the carrier-ecosystem migration. Decimal Tagged refactor cancelled; types remain standalone. Inventory v1.1.0 Phase 4 entry deferred-then-cancelled per this analysis. Codification queued: Recommendation #8 in capability-lift-pattern.md v1.3.0.

documents.topic: Decision rationale for deleting `swift-lifetime-primitives` from the workspace. Per-type analysis: `Lifetime.Lease` redundant with `Ownership.Unique` (weaker semantics, runtime trap vs compile-time linear); `Lifetime.Disposable` symbolic protocol with no conformers; `Lifetime.Scoped` is `defer` in struct form. Documents the mechanical deletion (workspace files, 5 packages with commented-out scaffolding deps stripped). Cross-references the second-arc reflection.

documents.topic: Decision (Option C, 2026-05-04) to add Cardinal.`Protocol` upstream in swift-cardinal-primitives — sibling to Carrier (not refinement), mirroring the Ordinal.`Protocol` precedent — to unify bare `Cardinal` and `Tagged<Tag, Cardinal>` under one constraint. Resolves the bare-vs-Carrier overload split pattern observed across six packages (swift-ordinal-primitives, swift-affine-primitives, swift-cyclic-primitives, swift-sequence-primitives, swift-finite-primitives, swift-bit-vector-primitives) after the Tagged `46ded75` cascade-drop. The memo body originally proposed an empty-marker `Carrier.`Protocol` where Underlying == UInt` shape; correction at top: that does not compile because `Tagged<Tag, Cardinal>.Underlying == Cardinal` post-cascade-drop. The working shape is the sibling pattern with its own `var cardinal: Cardinal { get }` accessor. Vector.`Protocol` deferred to L3 evidence accumulation. documents.topic: ci/cache

Investigation of architectural options for replacing regex-based SwiftLint custom rules with AST-based / semantic-shape-based enforcement in the swift-primitives ecosystem. Triggered by the R1–R4 cardinal/ordinal/vector enforcement-cohort cleanup (waves 1+2a+2b+2a-rerun, 50 sites across 21 packages) which surfaced 5 demonstrated regex-evasion classes (paren-wrap caught by companion regex 7622a8b, typename-swap caught by broadened R4 regex 7622a8b, cast-outside + algebraic-flip caught by companion regex c252a39, operand-reorder NOT catchable by clean regex — supervisor diff-review is the current gate, comments-as-code workaround-only via Unicode minus discipline) plus 2 deferred AST-shaped rule classes from cardinal-ordinal-vector-enforcement-design.md (R5 __unchecked: at call-sites only requiring call-vs-decl AST distinction, R0 positive enforcement of [CONV-016] preference hierarchy requiring conversion-tier classification on AST) plus 2 further deferred rules from rollout-phase-1-results.md v1.1.0 ([API-IMPL-005] one_declaration_per_file with per-path scoping for the [TEST-005] @Suite-category fixture pattern — SwiftLint built-in does not accept per-rule path scoping, only severity:; [TEST-025] test_support_module_in_tests with file-context awareness). Eight architectural options surveyed (A SwiftLint native AST custom rules via @SwiftSyntaxRule + Bazel build, B swift-format custom rules eliminated by closed catalog, C SwiftSyntax standalone CLI tool, D SwiftPM build-tool plugin, E SwiftPM command plugin eliminated as primary, F symbol-graph harvesting + post-processing extending the Phase-1-executed pipeline at swift-primitives/Scripts/{extract,distill,analyze}-symbol-graphs.*, G editor LSP eliminated as initial scope, H compiler-plugin macro eliminated as primary). Q1–Q6 sub-questions answered per dispatch brief: evasion-class coverage matrix, deferred-rule unblocking matrix, integration with the existing Tier 1/2/3 parent_config: SwiftLint chain, dev cost / payback per [RES-022] theoretical grounding (regex/AST gap as operational-semantics fact: regex matches surface text, AST matches grammar; 5 evasion classes correspond to lossy projections from grammar to text), per [RES-025] empirical validation via Cognitive Dimensions Framework on the recommended option (visibility/consistency/role-expressiveness all High; viscosity/error-proneness Low; abstraction Appropriate). Prior-art survey [RES-021] across Swift ecosystem (SwiftLint built-in rules, swift-format closed catalog, jpsim's swiftlint-bazel-example, swift-macro-toolkit, Periphery, SwiftFormat-nicklockwood, usami-k SwiftLintPlugin, stackotter swift-lint-plugin) and parallel ecosystems (Rust clippy + dylint cautionary, Go analysis package + golangci-lint as closest 'good outcome' precedent, ESLint user-authored AST plugins, Roslyn NuGet-distributed analyzers, Sorbet, Pylint/mypy/ruff cautionary on fragmentation). Recommendation: Option C (SwiftSyntax-based standalone CLI as new Swift package per developer-tool-package-architecture.md DECISION shape — standalone in Developer/, future-promotable to swift-tools/ superrepo) AUGMENTED by Option F (extend existing symbol-graph pipeline for disjoint signature-shape rule cluster). Six-phase phasing: Phase 1 scaffolding + R5 first rule (1 week), Phase 2 migrate R1–R4 evasion cluster including operand-reorder gain (2 weeks), Phase 3 R0 positive enforcement (3–4 weeks), Phase 4 [API-IMPL-005] per-path + [TEST-025] file-context (1 week), Phase 5 ~30 complex multi-decl rules from mechanical-rule-tool-classification-swift-primitives.md (3–6 months incremental), Phase 6 parallel symbol-graph extension per ai-context-reduction-via-type-system-tooling.md Phase 2 (2–4 weeks). Top risks: distribution friction (mitigated by Periphery/SwiftFormat precedent), rule-predicate precision especially R0 (mitigated by dual-fire warning window), SwiftSyntax version drift (institute already pins via macro packages), scope creep into closed-form regex rules (process discipline). Refines the prior 'no 5th bucket' finding from mechanical-rule-tool-classification-swift-primitives.md v1.0.0: not contradicted as classification-time reasoning, but extended with empirical sub-classification within swiftlint-custom into Tier 5a (regex-feasible: stay in SwiftLint regex — Foundation imports, L1 platform conditionals, filename conventions) and Tier 5b (regex-fragile, AST-feasible: move to new linter — R0–R5, [API-IMPL-005] per-path, [TEST-025] file-context, ~30 complex multi-decl rules).
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0. Tier 2 swift-primitives-scoped investigation responding to empirical regex-evasion accumulation observed during R1–R4 cohort cleanup (5 demonstrated evasion classes + 1 partial + 4 deferred AST-shaped rules). Internal grep [RES-019] complete (4 cited prior research docs read in full at write time: ai-context-reduction-via-type-system-tooling.md, cardinal-ordinal-vector-enforcement-design.md, mechanical-rule-tool-classification-swift-primitives.md, rollout-phase-1-results.md). Eight options surveyed; A/B/E/G/H eliminated on technical or scope grounds before comparison; viable shortlist A/C/D/F compared on evasion-class coverage, deferred-rule unblocking, integration, dev cost, ecosystem precedent. Tier-2 rigor satisfied: prior-art survey [RES-021] across Swift + 5 parallel ecosystems with contextualization-into-our-ecosystem step; theoretical grounding [RES-022] via operational-semantics view of regex/AST gap; empirical validation [RES-025] via Cognitive Dimensions Framework on the recommended option. Recommendation Option C (SwiftSyntax standalone CLI augmented by Option F symbol-graph extension) gated by 6-phase phasing with explicit out-of-scope items (no SwiftLint replacement, no swift-format migration, no editor/LSP integration in initial scope, no compiler-plugin/macro replacement, rule-name finalization deferred to implementation dispatch, no generalization beyond swift-primitives in v1.0.0). documents.topic: IMPLEMENTED (Shape γ) (v1.4.0, 2026-05-17): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide systematic-literature-review arc on cursor abstractions across the institute L1 ecosystem. Closed under Principal Authorization 2026-05-17 — choice C: Shape γ as the immediate move (Phases 0-3 — W2 unification only; W1/W3 stay in current packages) WITH Shape ι expansion explicitly scheduled as a committed Phase 4 follow-on arc (W1/W3 migrate to cursor-primitives). Phases 0-3 LANDED 2026-05-17 across swift-primitives/swift-cursor-primitives (new repo, commits 0f57273 + 2bd7800), swift-primitives/swift-binary-parser-primitives (commit 13e4dbf2), swift-primitives/swift-lexer-primitives (commit 575e4cb), and swift-institute/Experiments (BENCH-011 probe at cursor-span-bench-011, commit 3180870). Phase 0 BENCH-011 gate GREEN: Text path parity (Lexer.Scanner already used Tagged<Text, Ordinal>); Binary path 20-100× faster, attributed to legacy `public var position: Int` storage preventing @inlinable method optimization (root cause documented in §Implementation Notes). Phase 3 termination gate clean across 7 packages — workspace grep zero residuals on old shapes; ecosystem build green including swift-foundations/swift-json's 216 tests passing (load-bearing Lexer.Scanner consumer). API surface as implemented adds seek(to:) to the original peek/peek(at:)/advance/advance(by:)/consume/isAtEnd/count/position enumeration — class-(c) judgment within signature-latitude for parser-machine backtracking support. Count retrofit (Int → Tagged<Byte, Cardinal>) not in original brief; source-transparency verified by build + workspace grep (no external typed-arithmetic on view.count). Phase 4 Shape ι expansion remains pending — gated on HANDOFF.md Wave 1 Item 2 settling; sole technical fallback voiding ι (BENCH-011 unmitigable regression) did not fire. Inventory at HEAD 2026-05-17 surfaced six cursor-like primitives at L1: Binary.Bytes.Input (W3: Copyable+Sendable, [UInt8], Index<UInt8>), Binary.Cursor<Storage> (W1 rw: ~Copyable, Escapable, dual Index<Storage>), Binary.Reader<Storage> (W1 ro: ~Copyable, Escapable, single Index<Storage>), Binary.Bytes.Input.View (W2: now Cursor.Span<Byte> typealias post-Phase-2a), Lexer.Scanner (W2: now thin wrapper around Cursor.Span<Text> + Text.Location.Tracker overlay post-Phase-2b), Lexer.Pull.Stream<Tokens> (~Copyable & ~Escapable, wraps Lexer.Scanner). Four-axis decomposition (ownership × mutability × storage × position-type) identifies three structural clusters fixed by Swift's type system. Key finding: SE-0503 Accepted; Parser.Protocol declares associatedtype Input: ~Copyable & ~Escapable at swift-parser-primitives Parser.Parser.swift:90-95; ~10 institute protocols use the pattern; 2026-01-19 Lifetime Dependent Borrowed Cursors.md doc's structural-constraint claim is OBSOLETED.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-18): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide successor arc to cursor-abstractions-l1-ecosystem.md v1.4.0. Revisits the Three-Worlds decision with the empirical finding that the v1.4.0 doc's Shape A rejection reasoning ('Swift offers no way to make Escapable conditional on a generic parameter') is empirically false. Tagged precedent + the cursor-shape-a-feasibility experiment (commit fa990a4, V1/V2/V5/V6/V7 CONFIRMED) refute the literal claim; the narrower true constraint (no Mode-discriminator conditional conformance per V3/V4 diagnostic) doesn't block Shape A because Storage-as-borrow-carrier (Ownership.Borrow.\`Protocol\` framework from ownership-borrow-protocol-unification.md v1.0.0; Pattern 1 borrow-view per nested-view-vs-borrowed-naming.md v1.2.0) sidesteps the discriminator entirely. Recommends transition to Shape A as canonical L1 cursor architecture, conditional on (a) creating Byte.Borrowed: ~Copyable, ~Escapable, Ownership.Borrow.\`Protocol\` (Case B conformer parallel to String.Borrowed) in swift-byte-primitives, and (b) BENCH-011 replay clearing GREEN under generic-Storage indirection. Migration cost bounded (3 commits, ~6 files); pre-1.0-permissible per [ARCH-LAYER-008]. documents.displayName: Integration-Shape vs Substrate Cost — Skill-Amendment Proposal
documents.topic: Typed-primitive substrate cost claims (e.g., [INFRA-003]'s 'compiles to identical assembly', [CONV-010]'s 'typed-arithmetic is zero-cost') apply at the substrate level — per-operation, in isolation. They do NOT bound integration-shape cost — where on the call graph the substrate operations fire. The streaming-deserialize regression arc demonstrated the gap concretely: T-1 moved position-work from cold (error) path to hot (per-byte / per-token) path; substrate-level zero-cost claim was satisfied; +25% measured regression still fired. Proposes three composing amendments: (1) clarifying note on [INFRA-003] body in existing-infrastructure skill; (2) new [BENCH-012]/[BENCH-011a] companion in benchmark skill extending integration-probe trigger to call-graph-shape migrations; (3) audit-skill checklist requiring zero-cost-prior citations to be paired with hot-path integration assertion. Each amendment is small; together they cover the failure mode at three points (substrate citation, measurement gate, audit gate).

documents.displayName: Byte Primitive Extraction and Domain Naming
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.1, 2026-05-15): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide architectural decisions cemented from the 2026-05-15 byte extraction arc. Four decisions: (1) LargerDomain.Subdomain naming ordering — subject owns namespace when role/specialization at intersection; formalized across Byte × Parser and prior ASCII × Parser instances; promoted to code-surface [API-NAME-001b]. (2) Byte location split — Byte value type in swift-byte-primitives (zero parser deps); Byte.Input typealias in swift-byte-parser-primitives sibling L1 package. (3) Input is peer-foundational — deleted ~148 Parser.Input.* re-exports; consumers reference Input_Primitives.Input.X directly; verified 7 non-parser direct L1 consumers of swift-input-primitives (binary-coder, infinite, dictionary, list, queue, heap, terminal). (4) Byte.Protocol as refinement of Carrier.Protocol<UInt8> + stdlib basics (Sendable/Equatable/Hashable/Comparable/ExpressibleByIntegerLiteral); UInt8 conformance question deferred to capability-marker Tier 3 branching session. Plus Parser.Input qualification cost-IS-the-contract framing (extension Parser scopes now require Input_Primitives.Input.X qualification — verbose by design; surfaces architectural meaning syntactically). Binary-stack audit (binary-primitives, binary-parser-primitives, binary-base-primitives, binary-coder-primitives, binary-leb128-primitives) returned GREEN — no byte reimplementation across all five packages. Capability-marker generalization deferred to HANDOFF-byte-protocol-capability-marker.md. Binary.Bytes.Input vs Byte.Input unification subsumed by byte-cursor-primitive-unification.md v1.1.0 [RES-018] case (a) DEFERRED.

documents.displayName: Byte.Protocol as Capability Marker — Generalization, UInt8 Conformance, and Meta-Pattern Status
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.1.0, 2026-05-15): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide research discharging the question deferred by byte-primitive-extraction-and-domain-naming.md v1.0.1 §Open Questions 4.B. Two sub-questions answered. Q1 (should UInt8 conform to Byte.Protocol?): NO — UInt8 MUST NOT conform; Byte.Protocol conformance restricted to institute byte-domain types (Byte, plus future ASCII.Code / Latin1.Byte / UTF8.Code_Unit / RFC-specific byte types) with recursive Tagged conformance via `extension Tagged: Byte.Protocol where Underlying: Byte.Protocol, Tag: ~Copyable`. Rationale: (a) semantic identity per [RES-029] — UInt8 is arithmetic-algebras carrier, Byte is byte-domain twin; (b) adjacent-type consensus — UInt does NOT conform to Cardinal.Protocol, Int does NOT conform to Affine.Discrete.Vector.Protocol; conforming UInt8 would be the first exception; (c) bundle-conflict cost — operator shadow on `<`/`==`/`hash`, API-surface broadening to all UInt8 sites, Carrier-composition pollution of Tagged<_, UInt8>; (d) author intent encoded inline at Byte.Protocol.swift file header. Q2 (is there a meta-pattern worth naming/promoting?): per-domain manual X.Protocol recipe IS the canonical pattern; promote to skill rule (proposed [API-NAME-001c] or capability-marker rule family) under code-surface; decline meta-protocol variant (blocked by [IMPL-102] — Swift overlapping-conformance rules force incomplete implementation; same verdict as capability-lift-pattern family) and generator-macro variant (hides §1.2.1's recursion-vs-refinement structural decision). v1.1.0 §1.2.1 names the recursion-vs-refinement constraint principle: refinement-of-Carrier X.Protocol blocks recursive Tagged conformance because Tagged<Tag, X>.Underlying == X (per Tagged's universal Carrier conformance), not the bottom-most carrier type. Sibling form is required when recursive Tagged conformance is needed or anticipated — true for every Group A capability marker. Tagged-recursive-conformance to Byte.Protocol landed v1.1.0 alongside the sibling-form refactor of Byte.Protocol; the refinement→sibling change preserves Q1+Q2 verdicts and flips only the Byte-specific sibling-vs-refinement judgment. Prior art surveyed: internal (property-tagged-semantic-roles Group A/B taxonomy and fibration framing; phantom-typed-value-wrappers-literature-study Tier 3 SLR; protocol-abstraction-for-phantom-typed-wrappers Tier 3 DECISION rejecting unified-protocol Option I; cardinal-protocol-unification-memo live-fire six-package precedent; carrier-walkback reflection establishing [IMPL-102]); external (Rust Book ch10-02/ch20-02 orphan rule + newtype pattern verified; HaskellWiki Newtype convention verified; Scala 3 given/using as scoped alternative; swiftlang/swift Integers.swift Numeric hierarchy verified).

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-16): Tier 2 cross-package structural-shape decision for ASCII.Code (the renamed ASCII.Byte) as it joins the Byte.Protocol capability-marker family. Naming pre-cemented to ASCII.Code per [API-NAME-003] specification-mirroring (INCITS 4-1986 'code'/'code point' terminology) — not re-litigated. Three shapes evaluated per [RES-029] semantic-identity-first framing: (a) standalone `struct ASCII.Code` wrapping `UInt8` + one-line Byte.Protocol conformance with `rawValue → underlying` rename folded in; (b) `typealias ASCII.Code = Tagged<ASCII, Byte>` with classification methods attached via `extension Tagged where Tag == ASCII, Underlying == Byte` (Memory.Address pattern); (c) standalone `struct ASCII.Code` wrapping the institute `Byte` (not `UInt8`). RECOMMEND (a) on three tiers: (Tier 1 semantic identity, dispositive) Byte.Protocol.swift:63–66 + byte-protocol-capability-marker.md §Conformers name ASCII.Code as a peer byte-domain conformer alongside Byte itself, parallel to future Latin1.Byte / UTF8.Code_Unit / RFC-specific byte types — not as a Tagged-phantom-wrapping; INCITS 4-1986 'code' identity does not encode a phantom-tag relationship. (Tier 2 adjacent-type consensus) Byte itself is `struct Byte { let underlying: UInt8 }` — direct UInt8 wrap + direct Byte.Protocol conformance; ASCII.Code as the next family member should take the same shape. (Tier 3 tiebreakers among structurally-defensible options) Shape (a) has one-step storage indirection vs (b)/(c)'s two-step; `extension ASCII.Code` reads naturally vs (b)'s `extension Tagged where Tag == ASCII, Underlying == Byte`; future `Tagged<X, ASCII.Code>` composition works naturally under (a) via the recursive `Tagged: Byte.Protocol where Underlying: Byte.Protocol` extension, but under (b) requires `Tagged<X, Tagged<ASCII, Byte>>` which protocol-abstraction-for-phantom-typed-wrappers.md §'No deep lifting' explicitly does NOT support. Shape (c) dominated by (a) on every axis. Verified inherited state from prior subagent (do NOT re-derive): ZERO workspace files use ASCII.Byte as a typed value; consumer surface = 36 static `ASCII.Byte.<const>` accesses in 4 terminal-primitives files (constants typed UInt8 deliberately for Collection<UInt8> comparison) + 1 rawValue access in swift-foundations/swift-ascii's ASCII.Byte+INCITS_4_1986.swift + 283 ephemeral `.ascii.X` accessor sites (constructed-on-demand, consumed-immediately, never stored — these recompile against the renamed type with ZERO source changes). Migration sketch: add `.package(path: "../swift-byte-primitives")` dep (transitively pulls carrier + tagged); rename ASCII.Byte → ASCII.Code in 4 source files; mechanical rename at 36 const sites + 1 typed reference + DocC. The deferred `rawValue → underlying` rename per swift-ascii-primitives/Research/ascii-primitives-rawvalue-underlying-rename.md retires IMPLEMENTED in the same commit (rename + reshape land together). [RES-018] case (b) carve-out — domain-owned vocabulary at L1, not gated. Default mode advisory; principal authorizes implementation separately.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0 (2026-05-16). Tier 2 cross-package per [RES-020] (cross-package consumers across swift-terminal-primitives + swift-foundations/swift-ascii + ephemeral-accessor consumers in swift-json, swift-standards stack, etc.; informal semantic commitment; medium cost of error reversible; several-releases lifetime). Discharges the structural-shape question for ASCII.Code as Byte.Protocol's next per-domain conformer. Predecessor arcs cited as constraints (do NOT re-litigate): byte-primitive-extraction-and-domain-naming.md v1.0.1 DECISION (cements Byte.Protocol; identifies ASCII.Code among expected conformers); byte-protocol-capability-marker.md v1.1.0 RECOMMENDATION (per-domain manual recipe is canonical; §1.2.1 recursion-vs-refinement constraint; ASCII.Code named alongside Byte in Conformers list); protocol-abstraction-for-phantom-typed-wrappers.md v1.4.0 IMPLEMENTED (per-type X.Protocol recipe; §'No deep lifting' constraint on nested Tagged relevant to shape (b)); ascii-primitives-rawvalue-underlying-rename.md (per-package, 2026-05-03 — pre-authorizes the rawValue rename, retires in same commit if (a) or (c) wins). Live-fire precedents cited: Byte (struct wrapping UInt8 + direct Byte.Protocol conformance — match for shape a); Memory.Address = Tagged<Memory, Ordinal> + Index<Element> = Tagged<Element, Ordinal> (typealias-over-Tagged precedents for shape b's pattern, available-but-not-mandated). Four open follow-ups: (1) recipe re-use confirmation for Latin1.Byte / UTF8.Code_Unit — handled by existing recipe, no new research; (2) constants typing UInt8-vs-ASCII.Code status quo preserved (out of scope); (3) hexValue/digitValue compound-name debt flagged for separate code-surface pass; (4) [API-NAME-001c] skill-rule promotion deferred to skill-lifecycle workflow. Per [RES-022] structural correctness drove tier-1; per [RES-029] semantic identity (IS-A) prioritized over cost; per [HANDOFF-013]/[HANDOFF-050] prior research + workspace-wide grep cited not re-derived.

documents.displayName: Tensor Primitives and Tensors Package Decomposition
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.1.0, 2026-05-16): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide architectural decomposition for two new packages — `swift-tensor-primitives` at L1 (owns the Tensor value type and intrinsic operations) and `swift-tensors` at L3 (composes L1 + L2 specs + other L1s) — providing the institute's runtime-shape n-dimensional array surface as a complement to the existing compile-time-shape `Linear.Matrix<Rows, Columns>` / `Linear.Vector<N>` primitives. v1.1.0 supersedes v1.0.0 on three dimensions: (1) layer-placement correction — v1.0.0 incorrectly placed the Tensor concrete type at L3; corrected to L1 per [ECO-003] (L1 owns irreducible types; L3 composes), matching every existing L1 package (Buffer.Aligned, Linear.Matrix<R,C>, Complex.Number<Scalar>, Tagged<Tag, Underlying>); (2) strict /code-surface pass — `Tensor.MutableView` (compound name [API-NAME-002] violation) corrected to `Tensor.View.Mutable`; typed throws [API-ERR-001] made explicit on every fallible operation; Swift.Error qualification [PLAT-ARCH-011]; hoisted-protocol pattern [API-IMPL-009] for Tensor.Layout.Protocol and Tensor.Axis.Protocol; one-type-per-file [API-IMPL-005] file layout enumerated; (3) strict /modularization + /platform pass — Core internal [MOD-001] + umbrella-`@_exported`-only [MOD-005] + Test Support spine [MOD-024] + fine-grained per-type default [MOD-026]; SE-0450 trait-gated cross-package conformance [MOD-014] for Linear.Matrix interop; full Swift 6.3+ SwiftSetting profile [PATTERN-005/006/007]; L1 platform-agnostic [PLAT-ARCH-008c]; L3 libm via L2 swift-iso-9899 [PLAT-ARCH-008j]. Analysis sections preserved verbatim from v1.0.0 per [RES-008]: Step 0 internal grep, SLR (Kitchenham), Prior Art Survey (seven parallel subagent surveys: NumPy + ndarray, PyTorch + TensorFlow, JAX + MLX, Eigen + xtensor, ggml + apple/swift-numerics, theoretical grounding, internal ecosystem reuse), Theoretical Grounding (multilinear algebra / monoidal categories / Dex / SE-0393/0398/0452 reach), Formal Semantics (T-INDEX, T-MULTI-INDEX, T-SLICE, T-RESHAPE, T-BROADCAST, T-EINSUM typing rules + soundness invariant), Ecosystem Reuse Inventory (forty-plus primitive packages with file:line citations). [RES-018] case (b) domain-owned vocabulary at L1. [RES-021] contextualization step performed per universal pattern. [RES-023] empirical-claim verification with file:line/URL citations dated 2026-05-16. [RES-027] six premises (verification spikes pre-implementation: InlineArray + ~Copyable interop, Memory.Alignment.alignUp signature, Affine arithmetic on stride types, Tensor.Layout witness compilation, ~Copyable/~Escapable view lifetime-bound, SE-0398 variadic pack for Tensor.Named) + seven directions (element-type enum vs generic, reshape semantics, lazy/eager toggle, autograd, einsum surface, sparse/ragged variants, Tensor.Storage as type vs witness). Strategic Swift position: value-generic `Rank: Int` (SE-0452) for compile-time rank, runtime shape/stride values, Dex-style named-axis overlay via type-packs (SE-0398) as opt-in variant. L1 swift-tensor-primitives target structure: Tensor Primitives Core (internal — namespace + addressing types + Layout/Broadcast/Storage witnesses + Tensor value type + Tensor.View + Tensor.View.Mutable + intrinsic operations + typed errors), Tensor Dynamic Primitives (rank-erased variant), Tensor Named Primitives (Dex overlay variant), Tensor Primitives (umbrella), Tensor Primitives Test Support. L3 swift-tensors target structure: Tensors Core (internal — libm bridge via L2 swift-iso-9899), Tensors Operations (transcendental element-wise + reductions + matmul), Tensors FFT (Cooley-Tukey + Bluestein), Tensors Einsum (parser + executor), Tensors Linear (SE-0450 trait-gated Linear.Matrix/Vector interop), Tensors Format (Format.Tensor style), Tensors (umbrella), Tensors Test Support. L2 swift-dlpack-standard deferred until concrete L4 consumer needs DLPack interop. L4 platform compute backends (Accelerate/MPS/BLAS) out of scope. Implementation gated on principal authorization. Skill-Conformance Audit Annex enumerates per-rule conformance for /code-surface ([API-NAME-001..014, API-ERR-001..006, API-IMPL-005..019]), /implementation ([IMPL-INTENT, IMPL-COMPILE, IMPL-000..108, PATTERN-009+]), /modularization ([MOD-DOMAIN, MOD-001..029, MOD-RENT]), /platform ([PLAT-ARCH-008c, 008j, 011, 022, PATTERN-001..008]).
documents.statusDetail: v1.2.0 (2026-05-17). Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. v1.2.0 promoted RECOMMENDATION → IMPLEMENTED on completion of L1 swift-tensor-primitives at /Users/coen/Developer/swift-primitives/swift-tensor-primitives/. Three doc-level amendments captured: (1) Layout naming refined from Tensor.Layout.RowMajor/ColumnMajor (v1.1.0; [API-NAME-001] violation — compound type names) to Tensor.Layout.Order.Row/Order.Column (v1.2.0; nested under Order sub-namespace; Strided retained as sibling, not member). Prior art survey covered NumPy 'C'/'F', C++23 mdspan layout_left/right/stride, BLAS CblasRowMajor/ColMajor, Eigen, ndarray; nested-Order option selected on combined institute-rule + term-of-art + readability axes. (2) Three structural deviations stamped (Swift generic-arity constraint forced these): Tensor<Element,Rank,Layout> → Tensor.Value<Element,Rank,Layout>; Tensor.Dynamic<Element> → Tensor.Dynamic.Value<Element>+Tensor.Dynamic.Shape; Tensor.Index<Rank> → Tensor.Index.Position<Rank>+Tensor.Index.Error. (3) swift-vector-primitives added to L1 deps for typed-throws-aware iteration (stdlib Range<Int>.forEach doesn't preserve typed throws). Implementation outcome: 51 source files, 5 SwiftPM targets, swift build clean, swift test 21/21 passing, swift-linter 0 findings (initial 207 → cleanup 5 → second-opinion remediation 0). Repository remains private and untagged (no .git directory) per principal directive. L3 swift-tensors not yet implemented; remains the next dispatch. v1.1.0 supersession scope: Layer Decomposition, SwiftPM Target/Product Decomposition, Outcome, Audit Annex naming references. v1.0.0/v1.1.0 analysis sections preserved verbatim per [RES-008]. Companion artifact tensor-primitives-implementation-follow-ups.md v1.0.0 captures three skill-amendment candidates + Vector forEach ceremony gap + cross-package observation, all DEFERRED.
